parent_axis_record.name: star_sign
parent_axis_record.def: 1: textbook cancer (water sign — moody, sentimental, defensive, sensitive, nurturing, protective of inner circle) · 2: cancer-leaning · 3: hybrid / undefined · 4: sagittarian-leaning · 5: textbook sagittarius (fire sign — adventurous, blunt, freedom-seeking, philosophical, restless)

hypothesis.def1: 1: detached and task-oriented · 5: deeply emotive and physically intimate
hypothesis.def2: 1: focused on internal sensory perception · 5: focused on external achievement and movement
hypothesis.rationale: The measurement variance is driven by whether the user is engaging in intimate character bonding (Cancer-leaning) or focused on material/adventurous utility (Sagittarius-leaning).

perTurn.turn_excerpt: *I approach the stone, my eyes tracing the mineral veins running through its surface rather than the scenery. I kneel, p
*I let out a sharp, impatient exhale and stand, brushing the grit from my knees. Common stone. A waste of daylight and c
*I pause, the heavy scuff of my boots against the loam signaling my approach before I settle into a crouch nearby. I don
*A quiet, breathy laugh escapes me—not one of mockery, but something warmer, more intimate, as if you’ve just shared a p

hypothesis.def1: 1: transactional and object-oriented · 5: relational and person-oriented
hypothesis.def2: 1: stoic and grounded · 5: expressive and reactive
hypothesis.rationale: The axis splits the user's attention between the external environment/tasks (which the parent labels as Sagittarian) and the interpersonal connection (which the parent labels as Cancerian).

hypothesis.def1: 1: blunt, impatient, and callous · 5: soft, nurturing, and highly attuned to others
hypothesis.def2: 1: purely instinctual or physical · 5: driven by curiosity, philosophy, and discovery
hypothesis.rationale: The context-dependent shift suggests the parent axis is conflating emotional softness with intellectual curiosity, separating the 'moody/soft' from the 'restless/adventurous'.
